## Top Recommendations from Audit

### 1. Mobile Performance: LCP 6.4 seconds ( Priority)
**Impact:** Over half of search traffic is mobile — a 6.4-second load time means most smartphone visitors leave before seeing the firm's message.

### 2. Stale Content: Last Updated September 2020 ( Priority)
**Impact:** Five-plus years without fresh content signals to Google that the site is inactive, quietly eroding rankings for the keywords the firm currently owns.

### 3. Missing Meta Descriptions & Open Graph Tags ( Priority)
**Impact:** Without meta descriptions, Google writes its own preview text — usually uninspiring. Missing OG tags mean social media shares show no image or custom headline.

### 4. No Client Testimonials on Website ( Priority)
**Impact:** The firm has loyal, long-term clients (8+ year relationships cited in Google reviews), but none of that social proof appears on the website where it could directly convert prospects.

### 5. Absent from Google Local Pack ( Priority)
**Impact:** The firm ranks organically but doesn't appear in the Google Maps pack — which sits above all organic results and captures the highest-intent local clicks.

## Digital Intelligence
*Source: Digital Intelligence Brief (Agent F — external research)*


### Reputation Signals

**Overall Sentiment:** Positive
**Google Rating:** ★ 3.7 (6 reviews)
**Praise Themes:** Warm and friendly staff, Professional and knowledgeable, Long-term client retention

### Associations & Affiliations

- **AICPA** — Logo displayed on Who We Are page
- **Michigan Society of CPAs** — Logo displayed on Who We Are page

### Social Accounts

- **Facebook**: Active · 337 followers followers — https://www.facebook.com/MKPCPA/
- **LinkedIn**: Active · 11–50 employees followers — https://www.linkedin.com/company/mckeown-kraai-plc
- **Instagram**: Low — https://www.instagram.com/mkpcpa/

### External vs. Website Niche Gap

**Externally Detected Niches:** Agriculture, Construction, Manufacturing, Business Startups
**Website Niches (from audit):** Agriculture, Construction, Manufacturing, Business Startups

**Unleveraged Credibility:**
- 30+ year firm history not prominently featured on homepage
- No on-site testimonials despite verified long-term client loyalty (8+ years)
- Professional memberships (AICPA, MI-CPA) appear only on the 'Who We Are' page — not in the site header or footer where prospects scan for trust signals

> **Gap Summary:** The firm's external reputation — positive Google reviews, active Facebook presence, and long-term client loyalty — tells a stronger story than the website does. The gap between what happy clients say externally and the absence of social proof on the site is the highest-leverage conversion opportunity available without any major rebuild.
